walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Ship Board
walkthrough.text: Let's set up Nemesis. First, unfold the ship board and place it in the center of the table. This is a large board showing the spaceship layout with rooms connected by corridors. Make sure everyone can reach the board comfortably, because you will be moving miniatures around it constantly.

walkthrough.title: Place the Room Tiles
walkthrough.text: Now let's populate the ship. Find all the room tiles and arrange them face-down in the designated spaces on the board. Each room has a specific location. The rooms include the Cockpit, three Engine rooms, the Hibernatorium, Laboratory, Surgery, Armory, Generator, and Storage. Leave them face-down for now. Players will explore and reveal them during the game.

walkthrough.title: Place Exploration Tokens
walkthrough.text: Find the exploration tokens. These are small round tokens that represent what you find when entering a room for the first time. Shuffle them face-down and place one on each unexplored room tile. When a player enters an unexplored room, they will flip the token to discover items, dangers, or nothing at all.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Ship Status Board
walkthrough.text: Place the ship status board near the main board where everyone can see it. This tracks three critical systems. The engine status shows which of the three engines are online. The coordinates track shows the ship's destination. And the destination marker shows whether the ship is headed to Earth or somewhere else. Set all engines to offline and coordinates to unknown. The fate of the ship depends on what players do with these systems.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Intruder Bag
walkthrough.text: This is the heartbeat of the horror. Find the cloth Intruder bag and the starting Intruder tokens. Place one Blank token, one Larva token, one Creeper token, one Adult token, and one Queen token into the bag. Shake it up. When noise builds on the ship, you will draw from this bag to see what nightmare crawls out of the darkness.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Intruder Miniatures
walkthrough.text: Find all the Intruder miniatures and organize them by type near the board. You should have Larvae, Creepers, Adults, Breeders, and the Queen. These will be placed on the board as encounters happen. Keep them sorted so you can grab the right one quickly when a token is drawn from the bag.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle the Event and Attack Decks
walkthrough.text: Find two important decks. The event deck controls what happens to the ship at the end of each round. Fires spread, systems fail, and Intruders move. The Intruder attack deck determines how aliens fight back when they strike. Shuffle both decks separately and place them face-down near the board.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Markers and Tokens
walkthrough.text: Set out the noise markers, fire markers, malfunction markers, and injury tokens in organized piles near the board. You will need noise markers constantly as players move through the ship. Fire and malfunction markers come out from events. Light wound and serious wound tokens track damage to characters. Keep everything within easy reach.

walkthrough.title: Place Escape Pods
walkthrough.text: Locate the escape pod tokens and place them in their designated rooms on the board. Each escape pod has two seats, and there are a limited number of pods. These are your lifelines. When things go wrong, and they will, the escape pods may be your only way off this ship alive.

walkthrough.title: Set the Round Tracker
walkthrough.text: Place the round tracker marker on round one. The game lasts up to fifteen rounds. If the tracker reaches the end, the ship self-destructs and everyone still aboard dies. This is your countdown clock. Every action matters.

walkthrough.title: Choose Characters
walkthrough.text: Each player selects a character. There are six to choose from, and each one plays differently. The Captain leads and commands. The Pilot navigates the ship. The Mechanic repairs systems. The Scientist analyzes alien weaknesses. The Soldier excels at combat. The Scout moves stealthily. Take your character's miniature, character board, and unique action card deck of ten cards.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle in Contamination Cards
walkthrough.text: This is crucial. Take the contamination cards and deal one face-down into each player's action deck without looking at it. Do not peek. Shuffle your deck thoroughly. This contamination card might be harmless, or it might mean an alien is growing inside you right now. You will not know until the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Deal Secret Objectives
walkthrough.text: Deal two objective cards to each player. Look at them secretly and choose one to keep. Discard the other face-down. Your objective is your personal win condition, and it stays hidden until the game ends. Some objectives are cooperative. Others require sabotage or betrayal. Trust no one completely.

walkthrough.title: Place Characters in the Hibernatorium
walkthrough.text: All players place their character miniatures in the Hibernatorium. This is where the crew wakes up. You are groggy, disoriented, and the ship's alarms are blaring. Something has gone very wrong. Each player draws their starting hand of action cards. You are ready to survive.

walkthrough.title: Lay Out Item and Crafted Item Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the item cards and crafted item cards into separate decks and place them near the board. Items are found during exploration and picked up in rooms. Crafted items can be assembled in the Armory. These decks represent the equipment scattered around the ship that might save your life.

walkthrough.title: Final Check and First Player
walkthrough.text: Ship board with face-down rooms and exploration tokens. Intruder bag loaded. Event and attack decks shuffled. Markers organized. Escape pods placed. Round tracker on one. Everyone has their character, action deck with a hidden contamination card, and a secret objective. Choose a first player randomly and prepare yourselves. The ship is dark, the corridors are silent, and something is hunting you.

summary.title: Lay Out the Ship Board
bullets: Unfold the ship board in the center of the table
Ensure all players can reach the board

summary.title: Place the Room Tiles
bullets: Arrange room tiles face-down in designated board spaces
Rooms: Cockpit, 3 Engines, Hibernatorium, Lab, Surgery, Armory, Generator, Storage
Leave face-down — explored during gameplay

summary.title: Place Exploration Tokens
bullets: Shuffle exploration tokens face-down
Place 1 token on each unexplored room
Flipped when a player enters that room for the first time

summary.title: Set Up the Ship Status Board
bullets: Place near main board, visible to all
Set all 3 engines to offline
Set coordinates and destination to unknown

summary.title: Prepare the Intruder Bag
bullets: Place into bag: 1 Blank, 1 Larva, 1 Creeper, 1 Adult, 1 Queen token
Shake bag — draw from it when encounters trigger
More tokens added as game progresses

summary.title: Set Out the Intruder Miniatures
bullets: Organize by type: Larvae, Creepers, Adults, Breeders, Queen
Keep near board for quick placement during encounters

bullets: Event deck → ship events at end of each round
Intruder attack deck → resolves alien combat attacks
Shuffle both separately, place face-down near board

bullets: Noise markers, fire markers, malfunction markers
Light wound and serious wound tokens
Organize in accessible piles near board

summary.title: Place Escape Pods

Each pod has 2 seats — limited total availability
First-come, first-served escape route

summary.title: Set the Round Tracker
bullets: Place marker on round 1

Round 15 end → ship self-destructs, all aboard die

summary.title: Choose Characters
bullets: 6 characters: Captain, Pilot, Mechanic, Scientist, Soldier, Scout
Take character miniature, character board, and unique 10-card action deck
Each character has unique abilities and playstyle

summary.title: Shuffle in Contamination Cards
bullets: Deal 1 contamination card face-down into each player's action deck
Do NOT look at it — shuffle into deck
May be harmless or infected — revealed at game end

summary.title: Deal Secret Objectives
bullets: Deal 2 objective cards to each player
Keep 1, discard the other face-down
Objective = personal win condition, kept secret until game end

summary.title: Place Characters in the Hibernatorium

Draw starting hand of action cards
Players begin the game together

summary.title: Lay Out Item and Crafted Item Cards
bullets: Shuffle item cards and crafted item cards separately
Place both decks face-down near the board
Items found during exploration, crafted items built in Armory

summary.title: Final Check and First Player
bullets: Verify: board, rooms, tokens, Intruder bag, decks, markers, pods, tracker
Each player has: character, action deck w/ contamination card, secret objective
Choose first player randomly — begin the game

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Let's learn how Nemesis works. We have woken from hibernation on a spaceship overrun by alien Intruders. Each of us has a secret objective that determines our personal win condition. Some objectives align, like getting the ship to Earth and surviving. Others conflict, like sabotaging the engines or ensuring another player dies. Trust is fragile, and betrayal can come from anyone.

walkthrough.title: The Two Phases
walkthrough.text: Each round has two phases. First is the Player Phase, where we take turns performing actions. Second is the Event Phase, where the ship fights back with fires, malfunctions, and Intruder movements. The game lasts up to fifteen rounds, and if we are still aboard when the clock runs out, the ship self-destructs.

walkthrough.title: Your Turn: Two Actions
walkthrough.text: You can play cards from your hand for their unique character abilities, or you can use basic actions that are always available to everyone. You can take the same action twice if you want. Let's go through the basic actions first.

walkthrough.title: Basic Action: Move
walkthrough.text: Let's start with the most common action. Moving lets you travel from your current room to an adjacent room through a corridor. Here is the catch: every time you move through a corridor, you place a noise marker in that corridor. Noise attracts Intruders. The more you move, the more danger you create.

walkthrough.title: Basic Action: Careful Movement
walkthrough.text: If you want to move without making noise, you can perform a careful movement. This costs your entire turn, both actions, to move just one room. No noise marker is placed. It is slow and expensive, but sometimes silence is the difference between life and death.

walkthrough.title: Basic Actions: Pick Up, Trade, Shoot, Melee
walkthrough.text: Let's cover the remaining basic actions. Pick Up lets you grab items in your room or take an item from a fallen player. Trade lets you exchange items with another player in the same room. Shoot lets you attack an Intruder with a weapon you are carrying. And Melee Attack lets you fight hand-to-hand, but it is desperate and risky, using a card from your hand as damage.

walkthrough.title: Character Action Cards
walkthrough.text: Each character has a unique deck of ten action cards with special abilities. The Captain can command other players. The Pilot can navigate the ship from the Cockpit. The Mechanic repairs engines and rooms. The Scientist researches Intruder weaknesses. The Soldier has heavy combat abilities. The Scout can move stealthily and scout ahead. Playing a card for its action discards it from the game.

walkthrough.title: Your Deck Is Your Life
walkthrough.text: Here is something critical to understand. Your action card deck is also your health pool. When you take wounds, you lose cards. When you play actions, you discard cards. When your deck and hand are empty and you need to draw a card, you die. Every card you play is a calculated risk. Every wound you take shortens your life.

walkthrough.title: Room Actions
walkthrough.text: Some rooms on the ship have special actions you can perform. The Cockpit lets you check or set the ship's coordinates and destination. The three Engine rooms let you repair engines, and you need at least two of three working to fly. The Laboratory lets you research Intruder weaknesses. The Surgery lets you remove contamination or treat wounds.

walkthrough.title: More Room Actions
walkthrough.text: Let's cover the remaining rooms. The Armory lets you craft powerful weapons from components. The Generator manages ship power. The Escape Pod rooms let you launch a pod and flee the ship, but each pod only has two seats. Plan your escape route before you need it, because when panic hits, everyone runs for the pods at once.

walkthrough.title: The Noise System
walkthrough.text: This is the core tension mechanic of Nemesis. When you move through a corridor, you place a noise marker there. When you perform certain loud actions, noise goes in an adjacent corridor. Each corridor has a noise threshold. When the number of noise markers in a corridor reaches that threshold, an Encounter triggers and something emerges from the darkness.

walkthrough.title: Intruder Encounters
walkthrough.text: When an Encounter triggers, draw a token from the Intruder bag. A Blank token means nothing appears, but an Adult token gets added to the bag for next time, escalating the threat. A Larva token spawns a small facehugger-like creature. A Creeper brings a fast, dangerous juvenile. An Adult is a full-grown killer. And the Queen is the most terrifying thing on this ship.

walkthrough.title: Combat Basics
walkthrough.text: When you fight an Intruder, combat is resolved with dice. Choose your weapon, ranged or melee, and roll the combat die. Results range from a miss to a hit to a critical strike. Intruders have hit point tracks, so you need multiple hits to bring them down. They are tough, they are fast, and they hit back hard.

walkthrough.title: Intruder Attacks and Wounds
walkthrough.text: Intruders attack during the Event Phase and when provoked. Their attacks cause light wounds or serious wounds, and some inject contamination into your body. Light wounds are painful but manageable. Serious wounds can kill you, especially if you take a second serious wound to the same body area. Characters can only take so many wounds before they die.

walkthrough.title: Contamination
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about the most insidious threat on this ship. Contamination cards get shuffled into your action deck when Intruders wound you. When you draw one during play, it does nothing immediately. It just clogs your hand. But at the end of the game, every surviving player must scan their remaining contamination cards. Some are false alarms. Others mean you are infected, and an alien bursts from your chest. You die, regardless of whether you escaped.

walkthrough.title: Removing Contamination
walkthrough.text: The Surgery room is your best friend. If you can reach it and spend an action, you can remove contamination cards from your deck. This is not optional, it is essential. Even if you successfully escape the ship, contamination can kill you during the final check. Get to Surgery early and often.

walkthrough.title: The Event Phase
walkthrough.text: After all players have acted, the Event Phase begins. First, draw an event card. These trigger ship-wide catastrophes like spreading fires, power failures, and Intruder movements across the board. Then add a new Intruder token to the bag, making future encounters deadlier. Then resolve fire damage to any Intruders in burning rooms. Finally, advance the round tracker.

walkthrough.title: Fire and Malfunctions
walkthrough.text: Fire can break out from events or failed actions, and it spreads during the Event Phase. Fire in a room damages everyone inside, player and Intruder alike. If fire gets out of control, it can destroy rooms entirely, making them impassable. Rooms can also malfunction from events or Intruder damage, disabling their special actions until the Mechanic repairs them.

walkthrough.title: Intruder Growth
walkthrough.text: The alien threat escalates relentlessly. The Intruder bag grows over time as new tokens are added each round. Early encounters are often mild, maybe a Larva or a Blank. Late game, the bag is packed with Adults and the Queen appears more frequently. Intruder nests can also appear in rooms, spawning additional aliens. The ship becomes more dangerous with every passing round.

walkthrough.title: Escape: Pods
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about how to get off this ship alive. The first method is escape pods. Move to an escape pod room and launch. Each pod has two seats, so two players can share one pod. But pods are limited, and if someone takes yours, you are stranded. After launching, your survival still depends on the ship's coordinates being set correctly.

walkthrough.title: Escape: Hibernation
walkthrough.text: For this to save you, the ship needs working engines, at least two of three, and a valid destination set in the Cockpit. If the engines are destroyed or the coordinates are wrong, you drift into the void and die.

walkthrough.title: Secret Objectives
walkthrough.text: Now let's discuss what makes Nemesis truly terrifying. Each player has a hidden objective. Some want the ship to reach Earth and everyone to survive. Others need to ensure the ship does not reach Earth. Some need a specific player to die. Others want to bring an Intruder egg specimen back to Earth. And some simply want to destroy the ship entirely. You never truly know who is on your side.

walkthrough.title: Self-Destruct
walkthrough.text: The self-destruct system can be activated from the Cockpit. Once triggered, players have a limited number of rounds to escape before the ship explodes. Anyone still aboard when it detonates dies. This is a nuclear option, sometimes literally necessary for certain objectives, and always a source of panic.

walkthrough.title: Game End Conditions
walkthrough.text: The game ends when all players have escaped, died, or the ship is destroyed. It can also end when round fifteen finishes, which triggers automatic self-destruct. There is no safe way to wait out the clock. You must act, escape, and survive.

walkthrough.title: Final Resolution
walkthrough.text: When the game ends, surviving players face three checks. First, the contamination check. Scan your remaining contamination cards. If any card is the infected variant, you die. Second, the escape check. If you used a pod, the ship's coordinates must be valid or your pod is lost. If you hibernated, the ship needs working engines and correct destination. Third, reveal your secret objective. If it is fulfilled and you survived all checks, you win.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: Nemesis has no point scoring. Each player individually wins or loses based on survival and objective completion. Multiple players can win. Zero players can win. It is entirely possible for everyone to die horribly. That is the nature of survival horror. Now let's get to the practice tutorial and see if we can make it off this ship.

summary.title: Game Overview
bullets: Semi-cooperative survival horror on an Intruder-infested spaceship
Each player has a secret objective — some cooperative, some traitorous
Win = survive + complete your secret objective

summary.title: The Two Phases
bullets: Player Phase → players take turns performing actions
Event Phase → ship events, Intruder bag growth, fire damage, round advance
Game lasts up to 15 rounds — round 15 end = ship self-destructs

summary.title: Your Turn: Two Actions
bullets: Perform 2 actions per turn from basic or card actions
Can repeat the same action twice
Card actions use unique character abilities

summary.title: Basic Action: Move
bullets: Move to adjacent room through a corridor
Place a noise marker in the corridor you pass through
Noise accumulation triggers Intruder encounters

summary.title: Basic Action: Careful Movement
bullets: Move 1 room with NO noise marker
Costs both actions (entire turn)
Essential for stealth in dangerous areas

summary.title: Basic Actions: Pick Up, Trade, Shoot, Melee
bullets: Pick Up → grab items in your room or from dead players
Trade → exchange items with player in same room
Shoot → ranged attack on Intruder (requires weapon)
Melee → desperate hand-to-hand (uses card from hand as damage)

summary.title: Character Action Cards
bullets: 10-card unique deck per character
Captain: commands, Pilot: navigation, Mechanic: repairs
Scientist: research, Soldier: combat, Scout: stealth
Playing a card for its action discards it permanently

summary.title: Your Deck Is Your Life
bullets: Action deck = health pool
Wounds and played actions reduce deck size
Empty deck + need to draw = death
Every card spent is a calculated risk

summary.title: Room Actions
bullets: Cockpit → check/set coordinates and destination
Engine rooms (3) → repair engines (need 2 of 3 to fly)
Laboratory → research Intruder weaknesses
Surgery → remove contamination or treat wounds

summary.title: More Room Actions
bullets: Armory → craft weapons from components
Generator → manage ship power
Escape Pod rooms → launch pod (2 seats per pod, limited supply)

summary.title: The Noise System
bullets: Movement and loud actions → noise markers in corridors
Each corridor has a noise threshold
Threshold reached → Encounter triggers, draw from Intruder bag

summary.title: Intruder Encounters
bullets: Draw token from bag: Blank, Larva, Creeper, Adult, or Queen
Blank = no Intruder, but Adult token added to bag (escalation)
Place matching miniature in the room
Bag grows deadlier each round

summary.title: Combat Basics
bullets: Roll combat die → miss / hit / critical
Intruders have HP tracks — multiple hits to kill
Choose weapon: ranged (safer) or melee (risky)
Combat is resource-intensive — fight only when necessary

summary.title: Intruder Attacks and Wounds
bullets: Intruders attack during Event Phase and when provoked
Attacks cause light wounds, serious wounds, or contamination
2nd serious wound to same body area = death
Characters have limited wound capacity

summary.title: Contamination
bullets: Contamination cards shuffled into your action deck
Drawing one during play = no immediate effect, but clogs hand
Game end: scan remaining contamination cards
Infected variant = death (chestburster), regardless of escape

summary.title: Removing Contamination
bullets: Surgery room → spend action to remove contamination from deck
Essential even if you escape — contamination kills at game end
Visit Surgery early and often

summary.title: The Event Phase
bullets: Draw event card → fires, power failures, Intruder movements
Add new Intruder token to bag (threat escalation)
Fire damages Intruders in burning rooms
Advance round tracker

summary.title: Fire and Malfunctions
bullets: Fire breaks out from events, spreads each Event Phase
Fire damages all occupants, can destroy rooms (impassable)
Room malfunctions disable special actions
Mechanic can repair malfunctions

summary.title: Intruder Growth
bullets: Intruder bag grows each round — more Adult/Queen encounters late game
Nests can spawn in rooms, creating additional aliens
Threat escalates relentlessly over 15 rounds

summary.title: Escape: Pods
bullets: Move to escape pod room → launch action
2 seats per pod, limited pods available
Survival depends on ship coordinates being valid
Plan route early — pods go fast

summary.title: Escape: Hibernation
bullets: Return to Hibernatorium → go into hibernation
Requires: 2+ engines working AND valid destination set
If engines destroyed or coordinates wrong → death

summary.title: Secret Objectives
bullets: Objectives: reach Earth, prevent reaching Earth, kill a player, bring egg to Earth, destroy ship, etc.
Revealed at game end — you never know who is on your side
Must fulfill objective AND survive to win

summary.title: Self-Destruct
bullets: Activated from Cockpit — limited rounds to escape before detonation
Anyone aboard when ship explodes = dead
Nuclear option for certain objectives

summary.title: Game End Conditions
bullets: All players escaped, died, or ship destroyed
OR round 15 ends → automatic self-destruct
No safe way to wait out the clock

summary.title: Final Resolution
bullets: Contamination check → scan cards, infected = death
Escape check → pod needs valid coordinates, hibernation needs engines + destination
Objective check → reveal and verify secret objective fulfilled

summary.title: Winning and Losing
bullets: No point scoring — individual win/loss per player
Win = survived all checks + objective fulfilled
Multiple winners or zero winners possible
Total party kill is a real and common outcome

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Nightmare
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through the first few rounds with four crew members: the Mechanic, the Soldier, the Scientist, and the Scout. We will show you how movement, noise, combat, and objectives work in a real scenario. The alarms are blaring, the lights are flickering, and something is moving in the corridors. Let's survive.

walkthrough.title: Starting Position
walkthrough.text: All four players start in the Hibernatorium. You each have your ten-card action deck with one contamination card shuffled in. You each have a secret objective that nobody else knows about. The ship is dark around you, with unexplored rooms in every direction.

walkthrough.title: Mechanic's Turn: Move and Explore
walkthrough.text: The Mechanic goes first. For the first action, let's move toward Engine Room one. Place your miniature in the adjacent corridor, then into the Engine Room. Place a noise marker in the corridor you passed through. Since this room was unexplored, flip the room tile face-up and reveal the exploration token. The Mechanic finds a useful item. For the second action, pick up that item.

walkthrough.title: Why Exploring Early Matters
walkthrough.text: The Mechanic moved toward an Engine Room immediately. This is smart because most objectives require knowing the state of the engines and coordinates. The ship cannot fly without at least two working engines. Early exploration reveals the map, finds items, and lets you plan escape routes before the Intruders close in.

walkthrough.title: Soldier's Turn: Move Toward the Armory
walkthrough.text: The Soldier moves next, heading toward the Armory to find weapons. Place a noise marker in the corridor. The Soldier enters the unexplored Armory and flips the exploration token. Good news: there are weapon components here. For the second action, the Soldier picks up what is available. An armed crew member is a safer crew.

walkthrough.title: Scientist's Turn: Head for the Laboratory
walkthrough.text: The Scientist moves toward the Laboratory. Another noise marker goes in the corridor. Notice how three players have now left the Hibernatorium in three different directions. Three corridors now have noise markers. The Scientist explores the Lab and uses the second action to begin research on the Intruders.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Everyone Splits Up
walkthrough.text: Wait. Let's pause and recognize something dangerous. All four players just scattered in different directions on their very first turn. Three different corridors now have noise markers building up. Nobody is close enough to help anyone else if an Intruder appears. In a horror movie, splitting up is how people die. In Nemesis, it is exactly the same.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Balance Splitting and Grouping
walkthrough.text: Splitting up explores the ship faster and spreads noise across more corridors. But it means nobody can help you fight. Grouping up provides safety in numbers but concentrates noise in fewer corridors, making encounters more likely. The smart approach is to move in pairs, not alone and not as a full group. Two players can cover each other while keeping noise manageable.

walkthrough.title: Scout's Turn: Careful Movement
walkthrough.text: The Scout is smarter about this. Instead of rushing into a corridor and adding more noise, the Scout uses careful movement, spending both actions to move one room with zero noise. The Scout slips silently into an adjacent room. This costs the whole turn, but the Scout is now in position without attracting anything. This is the Scout's specialty.

walkthrough.title: Round 1 Event Phase
walkthrough.text: All players have acted, so now the Event Phase triggers. Let's draw an event card. The card shows an Intruder movement, causing existing Intruders to shift rooms, but since none are on the board yet, nothing happens. However, we add a new token to the Intruder bag, making it slightly more dangerous. The round tracker advances to round two.

walkthrough.title: Round 2: The First Encounter
walkthrough.text: Round two begins. The Mechanic moves through another corridor to reach Engine Room two. A noise marker is placed. But this corridor already had a marker from an earlier event. The noise threshold is reached. An Encounter triggers. Draw from the Intruder bag. It is a Creeper. Place the Creeper miniature in the room with the Mechanic. The Mechanic is face-to-face with an alien.

walkthrough.title: The Mechanic's Dilemma
walkthrough.text: The Mechanic has a choice: fight or run. Fighting a Creeper without a good weapon is dangerous and wastes precious action cards. Running means leaving the Engine Room without repairing it, and the Creeper will still be there when someone comes back. The Mechanic decides to run, using the second action to move back the way they came. Another noise marker is placed. Running is often the right call.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Fighting Unprepared
walkthrough.text: Let's say the Mechanic had decided to fight instead. Without a proper weapon, the Mechanic would need to melee attack, which means discarding a card from their hand to deal damage. The Creeper has multiple hit points, so one melee attack probably will not kill it. Meanwhile, the Mechanic would still be in the room at the end of the round, and during the Event Phase, the Creeper would attack back, potentially causing wounds and contamination.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Run First, Fight Later
walkthrough.text: Combat in Nemesis is expensive. Every attack costs resources, cards, or ammunition. Wounds drain your action deck, which is also your life. Contamination can kill you even after you escape the ship. The lesson is clear: avoid combat unless you are properly equipped, outnumber the Intruder, or have no other choice. Running is not cowardice. It is survival.

walkthrough.title: Soldier Engages the Creeper
walkthrough.text: The Soldier has weapons from the Armory. On the Soldier's turn, they move to the room with the Creeper and spend the second action to shoot. The Soldier rolls the combat die and scores a hit. The Creeper takes damage. One more hit should finish it. The Soldier is equipped for this fight.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Ignoring Contamination
walkthrough.text: During the Creeper encounter, it managed to wound the Mechanic before the Mechanic ran. A contamination card was shuffled into the Mechanic's deck. The Mechanic shrugs it off, thinking they will deal with it later. But later might never come. If the Mechanic escapes the ship with that contamination card still in the deck, the final scan might reveal an infection. The Mechanic could survive fifteen rounds of horror only to die in the last five seconds of the game.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Prioritize Surgery
walkthrough.text: Contamination is the silent killer in Nemesis. You cannot see whether the card in your deck is the infected variant until the game ends. The Surgery room removes contamination, but it requires getting there and spending an action. Plan a trip to Surgery the moment you receive contamination. Do not wait. Do not assume you will have time later. The ship gets more dangerous every round, and the path to Surgery may be blocked by fire or aliens.

walkthrough.title: Scientist Checks the Cockpit
walkthrough.text: The Scientist has made it to the Cockpit. Using a room action, the Scientist checks the ship's coordinates. The destination is currently set to a random location, not Earth. The Scientist now knows critical information. If the Scientist's objective requires the ship to reach Earth, the coordinates need to be changed. If the objective requires the ship to not reach Earth, the Scientist might quietly leave things as they are.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Trusting Blindly
walkthrough.text: The Scientist announces to the group that the coordinates look fine and the ship is headed to Earth. But is that true? Nobody else can verify it without going to the Cockpit themselves. The Scientist might be lying to serve their secret objective. This is the heart of Nemesis: every piece of information shared could be true or a calculated deception.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Verify Critical Information
walkthrough.text: Never take another player's word on coordinates, engine status, or objective alignment. If your survival depends on the ship reaching Earth, verify the coordinates yourself or have a trusted ally do it. The five minutes it takes to reach the Cockpit are worth it compared to dying because someone lied about the destination. Trust is a resource in Nemesis, and it should be spent carefully.

walkthrough.title: Round 3: Things Escalate
walkthrough.text: By round three, the Intruder bag has grown. There are more Adult tokens in the bag now, and noise has accumulated in several corridors. The Scout discovers a nest in one of the rooms, which will spawn additional Intruders. Fire has broken out in a corridor from an event card. The ship is deteriorating around you. The window for safe exploration is closing fast.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: No Escape Plan
walkthrough.text: The group has been so focused on exploring, fighting, and checking systems that nobody has planned an escape route to the pods. Now the corridors between the crew and the nearest escape pod room are filled with noise markers and a burning fire. The direct path is blocked. The long way around passes through unexplored rooms that could contain anything. Always know your escape route before you need it.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Have an Exit Strategy
walkthrough.text: From the very first round, know where the escape pods are and keep a mental path to at least one of them. As fire and Intruders block corridors, your options shrink. Experienced players constantly update their escape plan. If your primary route gets blocked, you need a backup. If both routes are blocked, you are probably dead. Plan ahead, or die scrambling.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations: You Survived the Tutorial
walkthrough.text: Congratulations. You have walked through three rounds of Nemesis and seen the core systems in action: movement and noise, Intruder encounters, combat decisions, contamination management, ship system checks, and the deadly trust dynamics of secret objectives. You have watched the crew make five critical mistakes and learned from each one. The real game is longer, darker, and more desperate. But you are ready for it.

walkthrough.title: Before You Enter the Ship
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for survival strategies and threat management.

Second, reference the Appendix for quick definitions of all Intruder types, room actions, and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Stay alive.

summary.title: Welcome to the Nightmare
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 4 crew members: Mechanic, Soldier, Scientist, Scout
Covers movement, noise, combat, contamination, and objectives
Follow along even with fewer players at your table

summary.title: Starting Position
bullets: All players start in the Hibernatorium
Each has 10-card deck with 1 hidden contamination card
Secret objectives dealt — nobody knows each other's win condition

summary.title: Mechanic's Turn: Move and Explore
bullets: Move to Engine Room 1 — place noise marker in corridor
Flip room tile and exploration token (first entry)
Pick up discovered item with second action

summary.title: Why Exploring Early Matters
bullets: Most objectives require knowing engine/coordinate status
Early exploration reveals map, finds items, plans escape routes
Ship needs 2+ engines working to fly

summary.title: Soldier's Turn: Move Toward the Armory
bullets: Soldier explores Armory — noise marker placed
Finds weapon components, picks them up
Armed crew members handle encounters better

summary.title: Scientist's Turn: Head for the Laboratory
bullets: Scientist moves to Lab — third corridor gets noise
Begins Intruder research with second action
Three players now scattered in different directions

summary.title: The Mistake: Everyone Splits Up
bullets: 4 players in 4 different locations after turn 1
3 corridors with noise markers building up
Nobody close enough to help if Intruder appears

summary.title: Lesson: Balance Splitting and Grouping
bullets: Splitting = faster exploration, spread noise, but no backup
Grouping = safety, but concentrated noise in fewer corridors
Best approach: move in pairs for coverage + manageable noise

summary.title: Scout's Turn: Careful Movement
bullets: Careful movement = both actions to move 1 room silently
No noise marker placed — stealth positioning
Costs full turn but avoids triggering encounters

summary.title: Round 1 Event Phase
bullets: Draw event card — Intruder movement (no effect with none on board)
Add new token to Intruder bag (escalation)
Advance round tracker to round 2

summary.title: Round 2: The First Encounter
bullets: Mechanic's movement fills a corridor to noise threshold
Encounter triggers — draw from Intruder bag → Creeper
Place Creeper miniature in room with Mechanic

summary.title: The Mechanic's Dilemma
bullets: Fight: risky without weapons, wastes action cards
Run: leaves Creeper in room, but preserves resources
Mechanic runs — the right call when unequipped

summary.title: The Mistake: Fighting Unprepared
bullets: Melee without weapon = discard action card for weak damage
Creeper likely survives → attacks back in Event Phase
Wounds + contamination from counterattack = devastating loss

summary.title: Lesson: Run First, Fight Later
bullets: Combat costs resources, cards, and ammunition
Wounds drain action deck (your life pool)
Avoid combat unless equipped, supported, or cornered
Running is survival, not cowardice

summary.title: Soldier Engages the Creeper
bullets: Soldier has weapons from Armory — enters Creeper's room
Shoot action → roll combat die → hit scored
One more hit to kill — proper equipment makes the difference

summary.title: The Mistake: Ignoring Contamination
bullets: Mechanic took contamination from Creeper wound
Decides to 'deal with it later' — may never get the chance
Could survive 15 rounds then die at final contamination scan

summary.title: Lesson: Prioritize Surgery
bullets: Contamination kills at game end — infected variant = instant death
Surgery room removes contamination cards from deck
Visit Surgery immediately after receiving contamination
Path to Surgery may be blocked later by fire or Intruders

summary.title: Scientist Checks the Cockpit
bullets: Room action: check ship coordinates and destination
Destination currently NOT set to Earth
Critical info — Scientist now knows, others don't

summary.title: The Mistake: Trusting Blindly
bullets: Scientist claims coordinates are fine — but is that true?
Nobody can verify without visiting Cockpit themselves
Information shared could be truth or calculated deception

summary.title: Lesson: Verify Critical Information
bullets: Never take another player's word on coordinates/engines/objectives
Verify critical info yourself or through a trusted ally
5 minutes to reach Cockpit vs dying to a lie — worth the trip
Trust is a resource — spend it carefully

summary.title: Round 3: Things Escalate
bullets: Intruder bag has grown — more Adults in the mix
Nests spawning additional aliens in rooms
Fire breaking out from events, blocking corridors
Window for safe exploration closing fast

summary.title: The Mistake: No Escape Plan
bullets: Group focused on exploring/fighting — nobody planned escape routes
Direct path to pods blocked by noise + fire
Long way around passes through unexplored rooms
Always know your exit before you need it

summary.title: Lesson: Always Have an Exit Strategy
bullets: Know pod locations from round 1, maintain mental escape path
Fire and Intruders shrink your options each round
Keep primary + backup escape routes at all times
If both routes blocked, you're probably dead

summary.title: Congratulations: You Survived the Tutorial
bullets: Covered: noise, encounters, combat, contamination, trust dynamics
Five critical mistakes demonstrated and corrected
Real game is longer, darker, and more desperate — but you're ready

summary.title: Before You Enter the Ship
bullets: Check out General Tips → survival strategies and threat management
Reference the Appendix → Intruder types, room actions, game terms
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Noise Discipline
walkthrough.text: The single most important skill in Nemesis is managing noise. Every standard movement places a noise marker, and when markers hit the corridor threshold, something terrible emerges. Plan your movement carefully. If you need to cross multiple corridors, consider whether careful movement is worth the extra action cost. Move in pairs so you can share corridors instead of filling new ones. And remember that some character actions also generate noise, so read your cards before playing them.

walkthrough.title: Contamination Management
walkthrough.text: Contamination is the game's cruelest mechanic because it kills you after you think you have won. Every time you take contamination from an Intruder attack, a contamination card is shuffled into your deck. At game end, if any of those cards is the infected variant, you die instantly regardless of whether you escaped. The Surgery room is your lifeline. Visit it as soon as possible after being contaminated. Do not gamble with your life by hoping the card is benign. The odds are not in your favor.

walkthrough.title: Combat Economy
walkthrough.text: Fights in Nemesis are expensive. Shooting uses ammunition. Melee costs cards from your hand, which are also your health. Taking hits causes wounds and contamination. Every fight is a net negative for your resources, even if you win. The best fights are the ones you avoid entirely. Run from Intruders when you can. Use fire to clear rooms without spending your own resources. Only engage when an Intruder is directly blocking a critical objective or your escape route.

walkthrough.title: Deck Management
walkthrough.text: Your ten-card action deck is everything. It contains your special abilities, your health, and hidden contamination. Playing cards for actions depletes your abilities. Taking wounds removes cards permanently. When you cannot draw, you die. The key is efficiency. Do not waste powerful character cards on situations where a basic action would suffice. Save your best cards for critical moments. Think of your deck as a countdown timer that you control.

walkthrough.title: Trust and Information
walkthrough.text: Nemesis is a game of incomplete information. You do not know anyone's objective. You do not know the ship's coordinates unless you check yourself. You do not know what is in the Intruder bag until it is drawn. Share information strategically. If your objective is cooperative, being helpful builds trust that pays off when you need allies. If your objective requires betrayal, act cooperative until the perfect moment. Watch what other players do, not what they say. Someone heading to the Cockpit alone late in the game might be changing coordinates.

walkthrough.title: Escape Planning
walkthrough.text: Start planning your escape from round one. Know where the escape pods are. Keep a mental map of corridors between you and the nearest pod. Track which corridors have noise in them and which rooms are on fire. As the game progresses, your options shrink. Pods get taken, corridors get blocked, and Intruders patrol key areas. If you wait until you are desperate to plan your escape, you have already waited too long.

walkthrough.title: Ship System Priorities
walkthrough.text: Most objectives involve the ship reaching or not reaching Earth. This makes the Cockpit and Engine rooms the most strategically important locations on the board. Check the coordinates early. If your objective requires Earth, make sure the coordinates are set correctly and at least two engines are repaired. If your objective requires the ship to fail, subtly avoid helping with repairs or quietly change the coordinates when nobody is watching. Either way, knowledge of the ship's status is power.

walkthrough.title: Character Synergies
walkthrough.text: Each character has strengths that complement others. The Mechanic and Pilot work well together on ship systems. The Soldier and Scout make an effective combat and recon team. The Scientist provides intelligence about Intruder weaknesses that helps everyone fight more efficiently. Even if you do not fully trust your teammates, leveraging each character's specialty makes the group stronger. Just remember that cooperation is a tool, not a commitment. Use it as long as it serves your objective.

summary.title: Noise Discipline
bullets: Every standard move = noise marker in corridor
Corridor threshold reached = Intruder encounter
Consider careful movement (both actions, no noise) for dangerous areas
Move in pairs to share corridors instead of filling new ones

summary.title: Contamination Management
bullets: Contamination kills at game end — infected variant = instant death
Surgery room removes contamination cards
Visit Surgery ASAP after being contaminated — don't gamble
Even escaping the ship won't save you from infection

summary.title: Combat Economy
bullets: Fighting costs ammo, cards (health), and risks wounds + contamination
Every fight is a net negative — even wins drain resources
Run from Intruders when possible, use fire to clear rooms
Only fight when Intruder blocks critical objective or escape

summary.title: Deck Management
bullets: 10-card deck = abilities + health + hidden contamination
Playing actions depletes abilities, wounds remove cards permanently
Empty deck + need to draw = death
Save powerful cards for critical moments, use basic actions when possible

summary.title: Trust and Information
bullets: You don't know anyone's objective — incomplete information game
Share info strategically: cooperative = build trust, betrayal = act cooperative until the moment
Watch what players DO, not what they SAY
Solo Cockpit trips late game = suspicious coordinate changes

summary.title: Escape Planning
bullets: Plan escape from round 1 — know pod locations and clear corridors
Track noise, fire, and Intruders along your escape route
Options shrink every round — pods taken, corridors blocked
If you wait until desperate, you've already waited too long

summary.title: Ship System Priorities
bullets: Cockpit and Engine rooms = most strategically important
Check coordinates early — knowledge of ship status is power
Cooperative objective: ensure 2+ engines + correct coordinates
Sabotage objective: avoid repairs or quietly change coordinates

summary.title: Character Synergies
bullets: Mechanic + Pilot → ship systems, Soldier + Scout → combat + recon
Scientist intel helps everyone fight more efficiently
Leverage character specialties even with imperfect trust
Cooperation is a tool, not a commitment — use it while it serves your objective

walkthrough.title: Reading Objectives and Predicting Betrayal
walkthrough.text: Advanced Nemesis play begins with reading the table. Watch which rooms players head toward first. Someone rushing to the Cockpit alone may want to set or change coordinates. Someone ignoring engine repairs when the group needs them might have a sabotage objective. Someone staying suspiciously close to another player might have a kill objective. The objective deck contains specific cards, so experienced players can narrow down possibilities based on behavior. Never accuse directly. Instead, position yourself to survive regardless of who betrays you.

walkthrough.title: Optimal Betrayal Timing
walkthrough.text: If your objective requires betrayal, timing is everything. Betray too early and the group turns on you before you can escape. Betray too late and you may not accomplish your objective. The ideal betrayal window is when you are near an escape pod or the Hibernatorium, with a clear path out. Complete your sabotage, like changing coordinates or damaging an engine, and immediately move toward escape. The best betrayals look like accidents or go completely unnoticed.

walkthrough.title: Intruder Bag Manipulation
walkthrough.text: The Intruder bag is not random. It is a system you can track and influence. Count what has been drawn. If the Queen token has not appeared and the bag is full of Adult tokens, every future encounter is extremely dangerous. Use this knowledge to decide when silence is mandatory. Some characters and actions can add or remove tokens from the bag. The Scientist's research can reveal bag contents, giving the group, or just the Scientist, a critical information advantage.

walkthrough.title: Fire as a Strategic Tool
walkthrough.text: Fire is usually a disaster, but advanced players weaponize it. Fire in a room damages all Intruders there every Event Phase. Setting a controlled fire in a room with a nest can prevent future spawns. Fire also blocks corridors, which can be used defensively to channel Intruders away from your position or offensively to trap other players. The risk is that fire spreads unpredictably. Never start a fire without an escape route that does not pass through the burning area.

walkthrough.title: Escape Pod Economy
walkthrough.text: With limited pods and two seats each, escape pod management is a competitive game within the game. Early in the game, identify all pod locations and note which corridors lead to them. As players start escaping, remaining pods become more valuable and contested. If your objective aligns with another player's, coordinate to share a pod. If your objective conflicts, consider launching a pod with only yourself in it to deny others an escape route. An empty seat on your pod is a weapon against your enemies.

walkthrough.title: Engine and Coordinate Manipulation
walkthrough.text: The ship's engines and coordinates are the most contested systems in the game. Destroying engines is permanent and devastating. Repairing them takes specific character actions and time. Coordinates can be checked and changed by anyone who reaches the Cockpit. Advanced players monitor who visits the Cockpit and when. If you need the ship to reach Earth, repair engines and set coordinates, then physically block or monitor the Cockpit to prevent changes. If you need the ship to fail, subtly damage one engine and wait for events to destroy another.

walkthrough.title: Character Mastery: Soldier and Scout
walkthrough.text: The Soldier dominates combat with weapon expertise and heavy attack cards. An armed Soldier can clear corridors that other characters cannot, making the Soldier the group's designated Intruder hunter. The Scout excels at silent movement and room scouting, revealing dangers before the group walks into them. Together, the Soldier and Scout form an advance team. The Scout identifies threats, and the Soldier eliminates them. Individually, the Scout is the best solo escapist, and the Soldier is the best at forcing a path through hostile corridors.

walkthrough.title: Character Mastery: Mechanic, Pilot, Scientist, Captain
walkthrough.text: The Mechanic is indispensable for engine repairs and room restoration, making them a high-value target for players with sabotage objectives. The Pilot controls navigation from the Cockpit, becoming the gatekeeper of the ship's destination. The Scientist's research reveals Intruder weaknesses and bag contents, providing information that can be shared or hoarded. The Captain commands other players, which is powerful in cooperative play but suspicious when used to position players for betrayal. Master your character's unique timing and leverage.

walkthrough.title: Late-Game Survival Calculus
walkthrough.text: From round ten onward, every decision is life or death. Calculate the minimum actions needed to reach your escape and work backward. Do you need to visit Surgery for contamination? Add two to three rounds. Do you need to pass through a corridor with Intruders? Add combat actions or a detour. Is fire blocking your route? Find an alternate path now, not next round. The best late-game players make decisions five rounds ahead. They know exactly when they need to start their escape sequence, and they commit fully when the moment arrives.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Metagame
walkthrough.text: Nemesis rewards players who think beyond the single game. Develop a reputation across sessions. If you always betray, people will never trust you. If you always cooperate, your occasional betrayal will be devastating. Mix your play style to keep opponents guessing. Study which objectives appear most frequently and learn their telltale behaviors. The best Nemesis players win not just by surviving, but by making everyone else uncertain whether they are friend or foe until the very last moment.

summary.title: Reading Objectives and Predicting Betrayal
bullets: Watch which rooms players head toward first — reveals objective hints
Cockpit rush = coordinate manipulation, ignoring engines = sabotage
Narrow possibilities based on known objective deck composition
Position to survive regardless of who betrays

summary.title: Optimal Betrayal Timing
bullets: Too early → group turns on you before escape
Too late → objective not completed
Ideal window: near pod/Hibernatorium with clear path out
Best betrayals look like accidents or go unnoticed

summary.title: Intruder Bag Manipulation
bullets: Track drawn tokens — know remaining bag composition
Queen not drawn + full bag = extreme danger
Scientist research can reveal bag contents
Use bag knowledge to decide when silence is mandatory

summary.title: Fire as a Strategic Tool
bullets: Fire damages all Intruders in room each Event Phase
Controlled fire on nest = prevent future spawns
Fire blocks corridors — defensive or offensive positioning
Never start fire without an escape route that avoids burning area

summary.title: Escape Pod Economy
bullets: Limited pods, 2 seats each — competitive resource
Coordinate with allies to share, deny pods to enemies
Launching with empty seat = denying opponents escape
Track pod availability as players start escaping

summary.title: Engine and Coordinate Manipulation
bullets: Engine destruction is permanent — repairs take character actions + time
Monitor Cockpit visits — anyone can change coordinates
Cooperative: repair engines, set coordinates, guard Cockpit
Sabotage: damage 1 engine, let events destroy another

summary.title: Character Mastery: Soldier and Scout
bullets: Soldier: combat specialist, clear corridors, force paths through hostiles
Scout: silent movement, room scouting, best solo escapist
Together: Scout identifies threats, Soldier eliminates them
Soldier + weapons = group's designated Intruder hunter

summary.title: Character Mastery: Mechanic, Pilot, Scientist, Captain
bullets: Mechanic: engine repair + room restoration (high-value target for saboteurs)
Pilot: controls navigation from Cockpit (destination gatekeeper)
Scientist: research reveals Intruder weaknesses + bag contents
Captain: commands players (powerful cooperative, suspicious for betrayal)

summary.title: Late-Game Survival Calculus
bullets: From round 10+: calculate minimum actions to reach escape
Add rounds for Surgery, combat detours, fire rerouting
Make decisions 5 rounds ahead — commit fully when the moment arrives
Late escape planning = death

summary.title: Winning the Metagame
bullets: Vary play style across sessions — always-betray = never trusted
Study objective deck — learn telltale behaviors for each objective
Best players keep opponents uncertain: friend or foe until the end
Reputation is a long-term strategic asset

entries.definition: The weakest Intruder type. A small facehugger-like creature that is relatively easy to kill but can still inject contamination. Drawn from the Intruder bag when its token appears.
A fast, dangerous juvenile Intruder. More dangerous than a Larva, with higher hit points and stronger attacks. Drawn from the Intruder bag.
A full-grown alien killer with high hit points and devastating attacks. The most common serious threat in mid-to-late game. Blank draws from the bag add more Adult tokens, causing escalation.
A specialized Intruder that can create nests and spawn additional aliens. Extremely dangerous to leave alive, as it multiplies the threat level on the ship.
The most powerful and terrifying Intruder. Highest hit points, deadliest attacks. Only one Queen token exists in the bag. Encountering her is often a death sentence for unprepared players.

Drawn from the Intruder bag, a Blank means no Intruder appears this time. However, an Adult token is immediately added to the bag, making future encounters more dangerous. Early Blanks are relief; late Blanks are escalation.

A cloth bag containing tokens representing potential Intruder encounters. Started with 5 tokens (Blank, Larva, Creeper, Adult, Queen). Grows each round as new tokens are added during the Event Phase and after Blank draws.
An Intruder nest that can appear in rooms. Nests spawn additional Intruders during the Event Phase, increasing the alien population on the ship. Destroying nests is a priority to slow escalation.
Discovered through research in the Laboratory. Reveals vulnerabilities that make Intruders easier to fight. The Scientist character is best suited for this research.

Ship room where players can check and set the ship's coordinates and destination. Critical for objectives requiring the ship to reach or not reach Earth. Also the location for activating self-destruct.
Three engine rooms exist on the ship. Players can repair engines here. At least two of three engines must be working for the ship to fly. The Mechanic is most efficient at repairs.
The starting room for all players. Also the location for the hibernation escape method. Players can return here and enter hibernation if the ship has working engines and a valid destination.
Room where the Scientist and other characters can research Intruder weaknesses. Successful research provides combat advantages and may reveal bag composition.
Critical room for removing contamination cards from your action deck and treating wounds. Visiting Surgery is essential after being contaminated by an Intruder attack.
Room where players can craft weapons from components found during exploration. An armed player has significantly better combat options than an unarmed one.
Room for managing the ship's power systems. Power failures from events can disable rooms across the ship. The Generator helps restore functionality.
Room containing supplies and items. Exploration here often yields useful equipment for the crew.
Rooms containing escape pods. Each pod has two seats. Players use a launch action to escape the ship via pod. Pod survival depends on the ship's coordinates being correctly set.
Passages connecting rooms on the ship. Noise markers are placed in corridors during movement and loud actions. Each corridor has a noise threshold that triggers an Encounter when reached.
Basic action to travel from your current room to an adjacent room through a corridor. Places a noise marker in the corridor you pass through. The most common source of Intruder encounters.
Special movement that costs both actions (your entire turn) to move one room without placing a noise marker. Essential for stealth in dangerous areas or corridors near threshold.
Basic action to attack an Intruder in your room with a ranged weapon. Roll the combat die to determine hit or miss. Requires ammunition or a weapon card.
Desperate hand-to-hand attack against an Intruder. Discard a card from your hand to deal damage. Risky because it uses a card (your health) and puts you in close range for counterattack.
Basic action to collect items in your current room. Also used to loot items from dead characters. Items include weapons, tools, and other equipment found during exploration.
Basic action to exchange items with another player in the same room. Useful for giving weapons to combat-focused characters or sharing critical supplies.
Special action available only in specific rooms. Examples: repair engines, set coordinates, research Intruders, remove contamination, craft weapons. Requires being in the correct room.
Token placed in corridors when players move or perform loud actions. When noise markers in a corridor equal its threshold, an Encounter is triggered and a token is drawn from the Intruder bag.
The number of noise markers a corridor can hold before triggering an Encounter. Varies by corridor. Once threshold is reached, markers are removed and an Intruder token is drawn from the bag.
Triggered when noise reaches a corridor's threshold. Draw a token from the Intruder bag and place the corresponding miniature in the room. This is the primary way Intruders appear on the board.

Card shuffled into your action deck when wounded by an Intruder. Does nothing when drawn during play. At game end, scanned to determine if you are infected (death) or clean (survival). Removed at Surgery.

End-of-game procedure where all surviving players reveal and scan their remaining contamination cards. If any card is the infected variant, that player dies from a chestburster alien, regardless of escape status.
Minor injury from Intruder attacks or hazards. Causes penalties but is survivable. Multiple light wounds accumulate. Tracked with light wound tokens on your character board.
Major injury with severe penalties. Taking a second serious wound to the same body area kills the character instantly. Much more dangerous than light wounds.

Placed in rooms when fire breaks out from events or actions. Fire damages all occupants each round, spreads during Event Phase, and can destroy rooms making them impassable. Can be used strategically against Intruders.
Placed in rooms when systems fail from events or Intruder damage. Disables the room's special action until repaired. The Mechanic character is most efficient at clearing malfunctions.
Face-down token on each unexplored room. Flipped when a player enters the room for the first time. Reveals items, dangers, or nothing. Once flipped, the room is considered explored.

Your character's unique 10-card deck of abilities. Also serves as your health pool — wounds and played actions reduce it. When empty and you need to draw, you die. Contains your hidden contamination card(s).
Character with leadership abilities. Can command other players to take specific actions. Powerful for coordinating group efforts but also enables subtle manipulation if pursuing a betrayal objective.
Character specializing in ship navigation. Most efficient at Cockpit actions like setting coordinates and checking destination. The gatekeeper of where the ship goes.
Character who excels at repairing engines, fixing room malfunctions, and maintaining ship systems. Indispensable for objectives requiring a functional ship. High-value target for saboteurs.
Character specializing in Intruder research. Can analyze alien weaknesses in the Laboratory, potentially revealing Intruder bag contents and providing combat advantages to the group.
Combat-focused character with heavy weapon expertise and powerful attack cards. The designated alien hunter of the crew. Most efficient at clearing Intruders from corridors and rooms.
Stealth-focused character excelling at silent movement and room scouting. Can explore without generating noise as efficiently as other characters. The best character for solo escape attempts.

Hidden card dealt during setup that defines your personal win condition. Revealed only at game end. Examples: reach Earth, prevent reaching Earth, kill a specific player, bring specimen to Earth, destroy ship.
Optional objective variant for the semi-cooperative mode. Adds corporate interests on top of personal objectives, creating additional layers of potential conflict and betrayal.

Activated from the Cockpit. Starts a countdown that destroys the ship after a limited number of rounds. All players aboard when it detonates die. Used for objectives requiring ship destruction or as a desperate measure.
Action taken in an escape pod room to flee the ship. Each pod has 2 seats. After launching, survival depends on ship coordinates being valid. Limited pods create competition.
Escape method where you return to the Hibernatorium and enter hibernation. Requires at least 2 working engines and a valid destination set in the Cockpit. If either condition fails, you die.
End-of-game procedure verifying escape validity. Pod escapees need valid coordinates. Hibernating players need working engines and correct destination. Failed check = death.
Tracks the current round from 1 to 15. If round 15 ends, the ship automatically self-destructs and all players still aboard die. Serves as the game's countdown clock.
First phase of each round. Players take turns in order, each performing 2 actions. Actions include movement, combat, item interaction, card abilities, and room-specific actions.
Second phase of each round, after all players have acted. Draw event card, add Intruder token to bag, resolve fire damage to Intruders, advance round tracker. This is when the ship fights back.
Drawn during the Event Phase. Triggers ship-wide effects like fires spreading, power failures, room malfunctions, and Intruder movements. The primary source of escalating ship-level threats.
Rolled during combat to determine attack results. Faces include miss, hit, and critical hit. Combined with weapon damage to track Intruder hit points. Some weapons modify die results.
Used in certain situations to determine noise placement. Results indicate which corridor receives a noise marker, adding unpredictability to the noise system.
Equipment found during room exploration. Includes weapons, tools, and survival gear. Items provide combat options, room interaction bonuses, or special abilities. Dropped when a character dies.
Powerful equipment assembled in the Armory from components. Generally stronger than found items. Crafting requires spending an action in the Armory room with the necessary components.

Set in the Cockpit. Determines the ship's destination. Must be set to a valid location for escape pods and hibernation to successfully save players. Can be checked or changed by anyone in the Cockpit.

Where the ship is headed, visible on the ship status board. Must be set correctly (usually to Earth) for most escape methods to work. A saboteur might change the destination to kill hibernating players.

Tracked on the ship status board. Three engines exist; at least two must be working for the ship to fly. Engines start offline and must be repaired. Damage from events or sabotage can take them offline again.
